data.title: Digital Realty partners with Oracle to help companies accelerate hybrid IT, AI adoption
data.description:
Data center REIT Digital Realty (NYSE:DLR) announced on Thursday a partnership with Oracle (NYSE:ORCL) to help companies accelerate hybrid information technology and artificial intelligence adoption.

DLR shares were +0.66% pre-market to $180.42, while ORCL was 0.48% higher to $243.05.

data.title: Cenovus Energy preparing competing bid for MEG Energy - report
data.description:
Cenovus Energy (NYSE:CVE) is preparing a bid for MEG Energy (OTCPK:MEGEF), setting up a potential challenge to Strathcona Resources' (OTCPK:STHRF) hostile takeover offer, the Financial Post reported late Wednesday.

MEG Energy (OTCPK:MEGEF) has set a Monday deadline for companies to submit bids to buy the oil sands producer, according to the report, which also said Cenovus (NYSE:CVE) is believed to be seeking financing to support the bid, although there is no guarantee it will proceed.

If Cenovus (CVE) proceeds with a bid, it would represent a significant escalation in the battle for control of MEG (OTCPK:MEGEF), which began in May with Strathcona's (OTCPK:STHRF) announcement of a $5.9B cash and stock takeover offer.

MEG (OTCPK:MEGEF) urged shareholders to reject Strathcona's (OTCPK:STHRF) proposal and launched a strategic review of alternatives, including an invitation of potential rival bids for the company.

Analysts at RBC Capital have said Cenovus (CVE) would be "the most logical fit" to buy MEG (OTCPK:MEGEF), since both companies have operations in the same oil sands region in Alberta and could cut costs.

data.title: S&T Bancorp GAAP EPS of $0.83 beats by $0.02, revenue of $100.07M beats by $1.02M
data.description:
• S&T Bancorp press release (NASDAQ:STBA): Q2 GAAP EPS of $0.83 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $100.07M (+3.3% Y/Y) beats by $1.02M.
  • Strong return metrics with return on average assets (ROA) of 1.32%, return on average equity (ROE) of 8.91% and return on average tangible equity (ROTE) (non-GAAP) of 12.12% compared to ROA of 1.41%, ROE of 9.67% and ROTE (non-GAAP) of 13.29% for the first quarter of 2025.
  • Pre-provision net revenue to average assets (PPNR) (non-GAAP) was solid at 1.73% for both the second and first quarters of 2025.
  • Net interest income growth of $3.3 million, or 3.90%, and net interest margin on a fully taxable equivalent basis (NIM) (FTE) (non-GAAP) expansion of 7 basis points to 3.88% compared to 3.81% in the first quarter of 2025.
  • Total portfolio loans increased $98.1 million, or 5.02% annualized, compared to March 31, 2025.
  • Total deposits increased $28.0 million, or 1.42% annualized, compared to March 31, 2025.
  • Nonperforming assets decreased $1.1 million to $21.3 million, or 0.27% of total loans plus other real estate owned (OREO), compared to $22.4 million, or 0.29%, at March 31, 2025.

data.title: Blackstone in charts: Q2 fee-related earnings growth jumps Y/Y
PHINIA Non-GAAP EPS of $1.27 beats by $0.24, revenue of $890M beats by $30.16M
data.description:
• PHINIA press release (NYSE:PHIN): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.27 beats by $0.24.
  • Revenue of $890M (+2.5% Y/Y) beats by $30.16M.
  • The Company refined its expected 2025 net sales to $3.33 billion to $3.43 billion vs $3.33B consensus

  • The Company’s net earnings and adjusted EBITDA are projected to be $140 million to $170 million and $455 million to $485 million, respectively, with net earnings margin of 4.2% to 5.0% and adjusted EBITDA margin of 13.7% to 14.1%.

  • The Company expects to generate $160 million to $200 million in adjusted free cash flow. Adjusted tax rate is expected to be in the range of 36% to 40%.

data.title: Berkshire Hills Bancorp Non-GAAP EPS of $0.69 beats by $0.10, revenue of $113.67M beats by $0.33M
data.description:
• Berkshire Hills Bancorp press release (NYSE:BHLB): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.69 beats by $0.10.
  • Revenue of $113.67M (+4.6% Y/Y) beats by $0.33M.
  • 3% increase in operating revenue linked quarter, 2% decrease in operating non-interest expense.
  • 3.27% net interest margin, 3 bps increase linked quarter and 7 bps year-over-year.
  • 56.7% efficiency ratio; improved from 59.5% linked quarter.
  • 0.48% delinquent and non-performing loans to total loans.

data.title: IMAX Non-GAAP EPS of $0.26 beats by $0.03, revenue of $91.7M beats by $0.85M
data.description:
• IMAX press release (NYSE:IMAX): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.26 beats by $0.03.

data.title: Blackstone Q2 earnings top consensus, helped by growth in private wealth, credit and insurance
data.description:
Blackstone (NYSE:BX) Q2 earnings grew from the prior quarter and a year ago, beating the Wall Street consensus and reflecting the company's expansion, especially in private wealth, credit and insurance, and infrastructure.

Total assets under management of $1.211T at June 30, 2025, vs. the Visible Alpha consensus of $1.196T, rose from $1.168T at March 31, 2025. Fee-earning AUM increased to $887.1B from $860.1B at the end of Q1 2025.

Net inflows of $52.1B compared with inflows of $61.6B in the prior quarter. 

"Despite the volatile market backdrop, we grew earnings significantly and total assets under management increased 13% year-over-year to more than $1.2 trillion—a new industry record," Chairman and CEO Stephen A. Schwarzman said.

Q2 distributable EPS of $1.21, vs. the consensus of $1.10, increased from $1.09 in Q1 and $0.96 in last year’s Q2.

Blackstone (NYSE:BX) stock rose 2.4% in Thursday premarket trading.

Q2 fee-related earnings of $1.46B, or $1.19 per share, beating the Visible Alpha consensus of $1.36B, rose from $1.26B, or $1.03 per share, in Q1 and $1.11B, or $0.91 per share, in last year’s Q2.

Net management and advisory fees were $2.02B, up from $1.89B in the previous quarter and $1.78B a year ago.

At the end of the quarter, Blackstone (BX) had undrawn capital for investment, or dry powder, of $181.2B vs. $177.2B at the end of Q1. During the first quarter of 2025, the company deployed $33.1B of capital and recorded realizations of $23.4B.

Total expenses of $1.93B vs. $1.89B in Q1 and $1.63B in Q2 2024. Compensation and benefit expenses dipped to $1.42B from $1.43B in the prior quarter and climbed from $1.21B a year ago.

Q1 investment performance by segment (appreciation/gross returns):

  • Real Estate

    • Opportunistic: +0.1%

    • Core: -0.4% 

  • Private Equity

    • Corporate Private Equity: +5.1%

    • Tactical Opportunities: +4.1%

    • Secondaries: +6.6%

    • Infrastructure: +2.9%

  • Credit & Insurance

    • Private Credit: +3.0%

    • Liquid Credit: +2.4%

  • Multi-Asset Investing

    • Absolute Return Composite: +2.8%

Conference call at 9:00 AM ET.
